_status_note: verified_in_runner = isExactReschedule guard confirmed in source. The .update() vs .delete()+.set() branch is in production code; the runner only verifies the guard string exists, not that Firestore receives the correct call.
title: Modify booking: reschedule to available new time — must update in place
failing_behavior: When customer rescheduled, the old booking was DELETED and a new booking was CREATED with a different ID. This lost booking history and issued a different confirmation ID.
root_cause: Prior _submitDirectBooking always called .delete(oldDoc).then(.set(newDoc)). The isExactReschedule guard was missing — it needed to check draft.isModify && draft.existingBookingId before choosing update vs create.

conversation.content: I need to change my appointment to 3pm instead of 2pm

Sure! I'll move your Manicure with Tracy to 3:00 PM. Confirm — name is Jane, phone 4085551234?
[STATE:{"intent":"booking_request","services":["Manicure"],"staff":"Tracy","date":"2026-04-14","time":"15:00","name":"Jane","phone":"4085551234","lang":"en","pendingAction":"modify_booking","existingBookingId":null}]

precondition: biz._bookingState has name=Jane, phone=4085551234, staff=Tracy. pendingAction=modify_booking triggers isModify=true in draft.
expected_behavior: If existingBookingId is set: Firestore .update() called on same doc. If not: old bookings marked 'rescheduled', new doc created. Delete+set is never called on a known booking ID.
notes: isExactReschedule = !!(draft.isModify && draft.existingBookingId). When true, .update() preserves history. INVARIANT comment in code: 'use .update() NOT .delete()+.set()'
